label: Evaluation-driven development & release gates

node.label: Evaluation-driven development & release gates
node.definition: Evaluation-driven development makes tests, risk thresholds, regression suites, and release gates part of the iterative model and application lifecycle.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Evaluation-driven development & release gates is to connect Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics, Red teaming, abuse testing & threat modeling to downstream capabilities including Observability, tracing & evaluation harnesses, Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps.
examples: substrate for Observability, tracing & evaluation harnesses
substrate for Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps
limitations: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics, Red teaming, abuse testing & threat modeling.

dependencies.rationale: Evaluation-driven development & release gates measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics.
Evaluation-driven development & release gates measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Red teaming, abuse testing & threat modeling.
Evaluation-driven development & release gates measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Human evaluation & preference studies.
Evaluation-driven development & release gates measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring.
